sourceMovie.title: Living
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Living for the same reason it broke through in 2022: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Oliver Hermanus keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Father
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Living clicked for you, The Father (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Never Let Me Go
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Living clicked for you, Never Let Me Go (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Living?
faq.answer: Try The Father, Never Let Me Go, October Sky—each shares DNA with Living while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Living?
faq.answer: Living (2022) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Living worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
